title: rows in sys.statistics are not removed when a temporary table is dropped.
poc: DELETE FROM sys.statistics WHERE column_id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM sys.columns);
SELECT COUNT(*) as COUNT FROM sys.statistics WHERE column_id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM sys.columns);
CREATE TABLE tbl_normal (id int, name varchar(99));
SELECT type, width, count, "unique", nils FROM sys.statistics WHERE column_id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM sys.columns);
SELECT * FROM sys.statistics ON COMMIT PRESERVE ROWS;
SELECT name, type, "system", commit_action, access FROM tmp._tables;
SELECT name, type, type_digits, type_scale, "null", number FROM tmp._columns;
SELECT COUNT(*) as COUNT FROM sys.statistics;
key_bug_features: Temporary table drop does not clear sys.statistics entries.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE tbl_temp (id int PRIMARY KEY, name varchar(100), created_at timestamp DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO tbl_temp (id, name) VALUES (1, 'Test Entry');
SELECT * FROM tbl_temp WHERE id = (SELECT MAX(id) FROM tbl_temp);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sys.statistics WHERE column_id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM sys.columns);
